qa_pairs.question: When do the 2021 Resource Allocation Competition allocations come into effect?
qa_pairs.answer: Allocations from the 2021 Resource Allocation Competition come into effect on April 1, 2021.

qa_pairs.question: How long is the overlap period for storage allocations between 2020 and 2021?
qa_pairs.answer: There will be a 30-day overlap between 2020 and 2021 storage allocations, starting on April 1, 2021.

qa_pairs.question: How are storage quotas determined on a given system during the transition period?
qa_pairs.answer: During the transition period, the largest of the two quotas (2020, 2021) will be adopted on a given system.

qa_pairs.question: What should users do if their allocation has moved from one site to another?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are expected to transfer the data by themselves via tools like Globus, scp, or rsync.

qa_pairs.question: Who should users contact for advice on transferring large amounts of data, such as 200TB or more?
qa_pairs.answer: For large amounts of data (e.g., 200TB or more), users should contact technical support for advice or assistance.

qa_pairs.question: How are quotas handled for contributed storage systems during the 30-day transition period?
qa_pairs.answer: For contributed storage systems, the sum of the 2020 and 2021 quotas will be used during the 30-day transition period.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to quotas on original sites after the transition period if data has been migrated?
qa_pairs.answer: After the transition period, the quotas on the original sites from which data has been migrated will be set to default.

qa_pairs.question: What action may staff take if storage usage remains above the new (default) quota after the overlap period?
qa_pairs.answer: If usage remains above the new quota after the overlap period, staff may choose to delete everything.

qa_pairs.question: Can the storage overlap period be extended upon request?
qa_pairs.answer: Reasonable requests for an extension of the overlap period will be honoured, though an extension may be impossible or severely constrained if the original cluster is being defunded.

qa_pairs.question: What is the scheduler team planning to do on March 31 regarding the Slurm database?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler team is planning to archive and compact the Slurm database on March 31 before implementing the new allocations.

qa_pairs.question: Which Slurm commands might be unresponsive during the database archiving and compaction?
qa_pairs.answer: During the database archiving and compaction, sacct and sacctmgr may be unresponsive.

qa_pairs.question: When are 2020 allocations expected to be replaced with 2021 allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler team expects to begin replacing 2020 allocations with 2021 allocations on April 1.

qa_pairs.question: How might job priority be affected during the allocation cutover?
qa_pairs.answer: Job priority may be inconsistent during the allocation cutover, and default allocations may face decreased priority.

qa_pairs.question: What will happen to jobs already running in the system during the allocation cutover?
qa_pairs.answer: Running jobs will not be stopped.

qa_pairs.question: What might happen to waiting jobs attributed to an allocation that has moved or not been renewed?
qa_pairs.answer: Waiting jobs attributed to an allocation which has been moved or not renewed may not schedule after the cutover.
